chapter_context.summary: Jehu the prophet declares the ruin of Baasha's dynasty; Elah is assassinated by Zimri, whose seven-day reign ends in suicide; Omri establishes the Omrid dynasty and builds Samaria; Ahab begins his wicked reign.

outline.text: Jehu the Prophet Pronounces Doom on Baasha's Dynasty
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Jehu the Prophet Pronounces Doom on Baasha's Dynasty (16:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Elah's Brief Reign in Israel; Assassinated by Zimri
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elah's Brief Reign in Israel; Assassinated by Zimri (16:8-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Zimri's Seven-Day Reign; Omri Besieges Tirzah; Zimri Burns Palace
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Zimri's Seven-Day Reign; Omri Besieges Tirzah; Zimri Burns Palace (16:15-20), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: Civil War Between Omri and Tibni; Omri Reigns and Builds Samaria
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Civil War Between Omri and Tibni; Omri Reigns and Builds Samaria (16:21-28), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

outline.text: King Ahab's Wicked Reign in Israel; Hiel Rebuilds Jericho
outline.explanation: This section focuses on King Ahab's Wicked Reign in Israel; Hiel Rebuilds Jericho (16:29-34), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Kings.

key_themes.title: Wisdom and Folly
key_themes.description: Solomon's wisdom is the gift of God, but his ultimate moral compromise reveals that knowledge without obedience leads to folly.

key_themes.title: The Temple and God's Dwelling
key_themes.description: The construction of the Temple represents a physical home for the Ark, though Solomon recognizes that even the highest heavens cannot contain God.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 16, what spiritual choices are you prompted to make to remain faithful to the LORD?
How does this chapter show the difference between outward religious form and inward devotion?

cultural_historical.title: The Golden Age of Israel
cultural_historical.text: Under Solomon, Israel reached its economic and geographic zenith, dominating trade routes and exhibiting unparalleled wealth.
